theme_notes: Trump was loudly booed at NBA Finals Game 3 at MSG (Knicks lost 115-111, series now 2-1); Trump insisting an Iran deal is 'days away' while the Strait of Hormuz stays partly closed; Zverev won the French Open on Sunday; Pope Leo XIV addressed the Spanish parliament calling for moral renewal.
settled: Zero. Even the Strait of Hormuz is less complicated than this right now.
Balanced. The Knicks are up 2-1. Everything is fine. For now.
Clean slate. Pope Leo XIV calls this a moral renewal. Just pay the tab.
youOwe: Under fifty. Less alarming than Trump showing up to your home court.
Smaller than a Knicks Finals ticket stub. Pay it before Game 4.
Iran's in negotiations. You owe fifty dollars. One of these should be easy.
Zverev won in three sets. This is going five.
Your debt has entered ceasefire negotiations. Both sides are skeptical.
Pope Leo XIV called for moral renewal. You called for dinner. Someone has to pay.
Trump promised an Iran deal 'in days.' You've been promising this for weeks.
At three hundred, your excuses have their own diplomatic channel.
Hormuz is barely open. Your wallet operates on the same schedule.
The Knicks lost at home with the president watching. You're also losing at home.
Trump kept Knicks fans waiting two hours just to watch a loss. You relate.
You've officially outlasted the Iran ceasefire. Congratulations, I guess.
You're not a debtor. You're a geopolitical liability.
Iran has $24 billion frozen. Your partner's patience is similarly locked.
Trump got booed by 20,000 people at MSG. Your partner is doing that in silence.
The Iran deal might close first. Genuinely uncertain which is more likely.
At this amount, they're not your partner. They're your central bank.
You're making history. Not the first-sitting-president-at-the-Finals kind.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a Knicks Finals ticket. Still: principle.
Pocket change in your favor. Hormuz is less stable. Take the win.
Small, but real. Unlike the Iran deal timeline.
Zverev collected a Grand Slam. A hundred bucks shouldn't be harder.
Pope Leo addressed parliaments about dignity. Consider claiming yours.
Small debt, their court, their problem. Just like the Knicks at MSG.
Trump keeps saying the deal is 'days away.' Your partner uses the same line.
At $300 owed, you're not their roommate. You're their IMF.
At this balance, your partner's wallet is in active ceasefire negotiations with you.
A grand in your favor. Knicks Finals courtside territory. They owe you Game 4.
At this level, the debt has foreign policy implications.
Zverev closed the Open. Your account is still open. Close it.
Trump was booed by all of New York. Your partner should be next.
You're owed enough to fund a small diplomatic mission. Or just a long cold silence.
The Knicks lead the Finals 2-1. You lead this negotiation by more. Press the advantage.
Five grand and they're still texting 'I'll get you back.' Classic Iran-deal energy.
You are their sovereign creditor now. Act accordingly.
The first American pope demanded moral renewal. Forward him their contact info.
